title: aquilegia vulgaris monstr. corniculata
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris monstr. corniculata, also known as the monkey flower or alpine rose, is a rare and unique cultivar of the columbine plant, characterized by its twisted and curled flowers resembling a monkey's face. This plant is known for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Aquilegia vulgaris monstr. corniculata include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing, making it a popular choice among herbalists and natural medicine practitioners. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
